text.title: Agent Rules and Migration Safety
text.summary: Agent-critical rules for EF Core: version pinning, migration safety, SQL safety, and human-in-loop checkpoints.

rule.statement: Pin the EF Core major version in every prompt. EF Core 8/9 changed JSON column behavior, complex types, and primitive-collection mapping; EF 6 knowledge is incompatible.
rule.rationale: LLMs conflate EF 6 (full framework) with EF Core; generated code using DbSet.Find + synchronous SaveChanges semantics silently produces incorrect async behavior.

rule.statement: Use ExecuteSqlInterpolated for raw SQL, never ExecuteSqlRaw with string concatenation.
rule.rationale: String concatenation in ExecuteSqlRaw is a SQL injection vector; interpolated parameters are always bound as query parameters.

rule.statement: After generating a migration, always run dotnet ef migrations script --idempotent and review the SQL before applying. Block any migration that contains DROP, CASCADE, TRUNCATE, or RENAME COLUMN without human approval.
rule.rationale: Destructive operations in migrations are irreversible on production data. SQL review catches cascade cycles, missing indexes, and unexpected column drops before they reach prod.

example.note: This check is necessary but not sufficient. Human review is still required for any migration touching more than one table.

antipattern.description: Generated queries using FirstOrDefault(u => u.Email == email) without case normalization.
antipattern.reason: SQL Server comparisons are case-insensitive by default but Postgres is not; .ToLower() on both sides is required for cross-provider consistency.
EF SaveChangesAsync is already async I/O; wrapping in Task.Run adds a thread-pool context switch with no benefit and can cause DbContext threading violations.

antipattern.description: Adding audit fields (CreatedAt, UpdatedBy) ad-hoc in every entity's configuration.
antipattern.reason: Cross-cutting concerns belong in a SaveChangesInterceptor, not duplicated per entity. Duplication causes inconsistent implementation and missed fields.

text.reference: Migration generation + SQL review script with destructive-op detection.
